steps: Prepare fresh fixative solutions before starting with sample preparation Safety critical Pre-warm double strength fix to 37 °C (or the temperature at which the cells were grown)
Add an equal volume of pre-warmed double strength fix to the cells in medium and fix for 00:15:00 at Room temperature Safety critical
Replace double strength fix with 2 mL of single strength fix and leave for 24:00:00 at 4 °C Safety critical
Prepare solutions fresh before resuming the protocol Safety critical
Prepare graded acetone series and pre-cool in the fridge at 4 °C
Prepare graded photo-activated LR white series and pre-cool in the fridge at 4 °C
Remove fixative solution from the 35 mm dishes containing the coverslips and replace with 2 mL of 0.2 Molarity (M) HEPES, On ice Safety critical
Wash the coverslips in:
2 mL of 0.2 Molarity (M) HEPES, for 00:05:00 , On ice (1/3)
2 mL of 0.2 Molarity (M) HEPES, for 00:05:00 , On ice (2/3)
2 mL of 0.2 Molarity (M) HEPES, for 00:05:00 , On ice (3/3)
Transfer coverslips to metal dishes filled with pre-cooled 50% acetone On ice , and transfer to the AFS unit
Reduce the temperature in the AFS over 00:20:00 from 4 °C to -20 °C
Dehydrate cells stepwise at -20 °C in:
Pre-cooled anhydrous acetone for 00:10:00
Pre-cooled 70% acetone for 00:10:00
Pre-cooled 90% acetone for 00:10:00
Pre-cooled anhydrous acetone for 00:10:00
Infiltrate cells with a graded photo-activated LR White series at -20 °C in the dark Safety critical We suggest to cover the AFS lid with a box during infiltration to exclude light.
Pre-cooled 25% LR White for 00:10:00 .
Pre-cooled 50% LR White for 00:10:00 .
Pre-cooled 75% LR White for 00:10:00 .
Pre-cooled 100% LR White Overnight .
Make fresh photo-activated 100% LR White, pre-cool at 4 °C for 00:20:00 and then transfer to the AFS unit for at least 00:20:00 before use to cool further to -20 °C Safety critical
Infiltrate cells with the pre-cooled photo-activated 100% LR White for 02:00:00 at -20 °C in the dark
Polymerise resin:
Warm samples to 4 °C over 16:00:00 with the UV light turned off
Store resin blocks in the fridge at 4 °C

This protocol was developed for cell monolayers grown on glass coverslips in 35 mm dishes. Appropriate solution volumes are indicated. The protocol can be adapted for use on tissues by extending dehydration and infiltration times. For dehydration and resin infiltration steps in metal dishes, fill the metal dish at least half full so that the cells will not dry out, which would destroy the ultrastructure.
Place an Aclar disc (cut to size from an Aclar sheet) over the dish to exclude oxygen during polymerisation
If needed, top up the liquid nitrogen in the AFS, being careful not to decrease temperature below -20 °C Safety critical
Attach the UV lid to the AFS unit and turn on the UV power
Polymerise for 48:00:00 at -20 °C with the UV light on
Prepare the Automated Freeze Substitution (AFS) unit Safety critical Set the AFS unit to 4 °C and fill with liquid nitrogen

Dehydration and infiltration notes: Transfer pre-cooled 70% and 90% acetone to the AFS chamber. Also add an empty 15 ml glass bottle for waste. After each solution change, remove the waste, and transfer the next solution from the fridge to the AFS unit so that it can pre-cool to the appropriate temperature. Use pre-cooled metal forceps (orange insulated tweezers) to manipulate samples within the AFS unit. Ensure there is enough acetone in the dishes - do not allow the cells to dry out as this will destroy the ultrastructure.
creator: Dumisile Lumkwana
